golden.note: append 3 rows, merge-on-read positional delete of id=2, then time-travel back to the pre-delete snapshot. One observation per 'observe' entry in the op-log, in order. decoded-rows keyed by field-id (0=__rowkey, 1=id, 2=category); positional delete => delete-file content=1. NOTE: on the delete snapshot, total-records stays 3 (the physical data-record count; the delete is a separate file) and deleted-records is not set — this is the merge-on-read summary semantics, confirmed byte-identical across the iceberg-java reference and iceberg-go. An earlier draft of this golden used logical live-row counts (total-records=2, deleted-records=1); the reference settled it.

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec 1 — v2 append + merge-on-read POSITION delete + time-travel.
# Authored-golden mode (expect present). Green on iceberg-go: MoR delete writes
# position deletes today, which is exactly what this pins.
#
# NOTE: this is the position-delete case, NOT equality. Equality delete is a
# declared gap for iceberg-go (see supports.yaml) — a separate spec would exit 4.

header:
  id: v2_append_posdelete_timetravel
  format-version: 2
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#row-level-deletes"
  table-uuid: "9c12d441-03fe-4693-9a96-a0705ddf69c1"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,       type: long,   required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: category, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !long 1, category: !string "a" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !long 2, category: !string "b" }
      - { __rowkey: r3, id: !long 3, category: !string "a" }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: after-append
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }
          - { id: 3, category: a }

  - op: delete
    strategy: merge-on-read
    kind: position
    predicate: { type: eq, term: id, value: !long 2 }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: delete }
      delete-file: { content: 1 }          # positional (not a deletion vector, not equality)

  # rule: mutations assert snapshot facts; observes assert row-state.
  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: after-delete
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 3, category: a }

  # time-travel: the pre-delete snapshot still reads all three rows
  - op: observe
    at: after-append
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }
          - { id: 3, category: a }

golden.note: two appends (ordinals 0 and 1), then time-travel to the first snapshot. Universal-write baseline: create + append + scan + time-travel only, so every implementation runs it green (verified byte-identical modulo row order across iceberg-go and iceberg-rust). decoded-rows keyed by field-id (0=__rowkey, 1=id, 2=category); row order is not significant (the orchestrator sorts by __rowkey).

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec — v2 two appends + time-travel, NO delete. Authored-golden.
#
# The universal-write fixture: it uses only create + append + scan + time-travel,
# which every implementation supports. This is the cross-implementation baseline
# — iceberg-go AND iceberg-rust both run it fully green, so a divergence here is a
# real interop bug, not a feature gap. (The sibling v2_append_posdelete_timetravel
# adds a merge-on-read delete, which iceberg-rust cannot write yet -> exit 4.)
#
# Pins: snapshot ordinals across two commits (0, 1), the append operation, live
# row multiset after each append, and a time-travel read of the first snapshot.

header:
  id: v2_append_timetravel
  format-version: 2
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#snapshots"
  table-uuid: "7b3e1a02-9c4d-4f18-8a2b-6e5f0d1c2b3a"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,       type: long,   required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: category, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !long 1, category: !string "a" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !long 2, category: !string "b" }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: after-first
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }

  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r3, id: !long 3, category: !string "a" }
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: after-second
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }
          - { id: 3, category: a }

  # time-travel: the first snapshot still reads only the first two rows
  - op: observe
    at: after-first
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }

golden.note: read-conformance: load a checked-in v2 table (minted from v2_append_timetravel) and observe the current state. Collapses to the PR #2 read shape — a single observation, no write-side snapshots section. decoded-rows keyed by field-id (0=__rowkey, 1=id, 2=category); row order is not significant (the orchestrator sorts by __rowkey). All three implementations must decode the same bytes to this same logical result.

fixtures.yaml:
# Read-conformance fixture — load a checked-in v2 table and observe it.
#
# source: artifact means the runner does NOT build the table from an op-log; it
# materializes the checked-in bytes/ (restoring them to the pinned root named in
# bytes/ROOT) and loads the table read-only, then runs the observe entries. This
# is the read side of the same contract: the observe -> decode -> canonical-emit
# half is identical to the write (synthesized) fixtures.
#
# The bytes/ tree was minted by running fixtures/v2_append_timetravel through the
# DML runner into the pinned root — so this fixture and its write sibling share a
# golden's row-state, demonstrating that write-conformance mints read-conformance.

header:
  id: v2_read_append_timetravel
  format-version: 2
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#snapshots"
  source: artifact
  artifact:
    path: .        # bytes/ lives alongside this fixture.yaml

entries:
  # read the current table state (the two appends of the sibling write fixture)
  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, category: a }
          - { id: 2, category: b }
          - { id: 3, category: a }

# Spec 2 — ORACLE MODE. No authored golden. Correctness = a metamorphic relation
# that needs no source of truth: compaction must be a logical no-op on the live
# row multiset, and must materialize away the pending delete files.
#
# This is the headline "we catch compaction bugs with no reference engine" demo.
# Green on iceberg-go: MoR position delete + ReplaceDataFiles (rewrite).

header:
  id: v3_compaction_noop
  format-version: 3
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#compaction"
  table-uuid: "1f2e3d4c-5b6a-7980-1234-567890abcdef"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,  type: long, required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: val, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !long 1, val: !string "x" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !long 2, val: !string "y" }
      - { __rowkey: r3, id: !long 3, val: !string "z" }

  - op: delete
    strategy: merge-on-read
    kind: position
    predicate: { type: eq, term: id, value: !long 2 }

  # capture the logical state right before compaction
  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: before-compaction

  - op: rewrite          # compaction / rewrite_data_files

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    invariant:
      # the live-row multiset must be byte-for-byte the SAME set of rowkeys as
      # before compaction — compaction changed physical layout only.
      rowkey-multiset-equals: before-compaction
      # and the position-delete must have been applied and folded away.
      physical:
        delete-files-remaining: 0
      # cheap secondary oracle, no reference needed:
      count-equals-summary: true

golden.note: append, then promote id int->long and add column score (long) with initial-default/write-default=0, then append a row omitting score, then time-travel to the pre-evolution snapshot. iceberg-schema differs across observations (evolution). initial-default backfills existing rows; write-default fills the omitted column. Row lineage (_row_id) verified against the current v3 spec before blessing.

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec 3 — v3 schema evolution + default values + time-travel across the change.
# Authored-golden. Green on iceberg-go: UpdateSchema (promote-type, add-column
# with initial-default/write-default).
#
# Row lineage (_row_id / next-row-id) is expressed logically (__rowkey -> row-id)
# rather than by raw reserved field-id; VERIFY the exact reserved ids against the
# current v3 spec before blessing the golden.

header:
  id: v3_schema_evolution_defaults
  format-version: 3
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#schema-evolution"
  table-uuid: "aa11bb22-cc33-dd44-ee55-ff6677889900"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1, name: id,   type: int, required: true }
      - { id: 2, name: name, type: string }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r1, id: !int 1, name: !string "ann" }
      - { __rowkey: r2, id: !int 2, name: !string "bob" }

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    bind: v1

  - op: evolve-schema
    changes:
      - kind: promote-type
        field-id: 1
        to: long
      - kind: add-column
        field:
          id: 3
          name: score
          type: long
          initial-default: !long 0     # existing rows read as 0
          write-default: !long 0       # new rows omitting score get 0

  - op: append
    rows:
      - { __rowkey: r3, id: !long 3, name: !string "cid" }   # score omitted -> write-default

  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, name: ann, score: 0 }   # initial-default backfills existing rows
          - { id: 2, name: bob, score: 0 }
          - { id: 3, name: cid, score: 0 }   # write-default fills the omitted column
      physical:
        metadata.next-row-id: ">= 3"
        row-lineage: { r1: 0, r2: 1, r3: 2 }

  # pre-evolution snapshot reads the old schema and rows only
  - op: observe
    at: v1
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - { id: 1, name: ann }
          - { id: 2, name: bob }

golden.note: single append of two rows over a wide type surface, one observation. decoded-rows keyed by field-id; c_long above int32 range is a long (JSON string); nested struct/list/map are type-annotated trees; SQL-null is {type,value:null}. Partition transforms bucket[4](c_int) + truncate[2](c_str) produce two distinct partition tuples.

fixtures.yaml:
# Spec 4 — type + transform breadth. Single append, canonical scan. The biggest
# raw-coverage-per-line win: "does the writer serialize every type identically,
# and place rows in the right partitions". Authored-golden.
#
# Covers primitives incl. int-vs-long distinction, decimal, timestamp, uuid,
# fixed, binary, nested struct + list + map, and a bucket + a truncate transform.
# Variant is INTENTIONALLY OMITTED from the write side here — iceberg-go writes
# variant via arrow-go but variant WRITE support should be confirmed before
# pinning; the read side is already covered by PR #2. If variant-write is a gap,
# it becomes a supports.yaml omission, not a failure.

header:
  id: v3_types_transforms
  format-version: 3
  spec-anchor: "table-spec#schemas-and-data-types"
  table-uuid: "cafef00d-1234-5678-9abc-def012345678"
  seeds:
    snapshot-id: sequential
    commit-ts: "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"
    file-paths: templated
  schema:
    fields:
      - { id: 1,  name: c_int,     type: int, required: true }
      - { id: 2,  name: c_long,    type: long }
      - { id: 3,  name: c_dec,     type: "decimal(9,2)" }
      - { id: 4,  name: c_ts,      type: timestamp }
      - { id: 5,  name: c_uuid,    type: uuid }
      - { id: 6,  name: c_fixed,   type: "fixed[4]" }
      - { id: 7,  name: c_bin,     type: binary }
      - { id: 8,  name: c_str,     type: string }
      - id: 9
        name: c_struct
        type:
          type: struct
          fields:
            - { id: 20, name: a, type: int }
            - { id: 21, name: b, type: string }
      - id: 10
        name: c_list
        type: { type: list, element-id: 30, element: string }
      - id: 11
        name: c_map
        type: { type: map, key-id: 40, key: string, value-id: 41, value: long }
  partition-spec:
    fields:
      - { source-id: 1, field-id: 1000, transform: "bucket[4]",   name: c_int_bucket }
      - { source-id: 8, field-id: 1001, transform: "truncate[2]", name: c_str_trunc }

entries:
  - op: append
    rows:
      - __rowkey: r1
        c_int:    !int 1
        c_long:   !long 10000000000            # > int32 range: pins long, not int
        c_dec:    !decimal "12.34"
        c_ts:     !timestamp "2026-01-01T12:00:00"
        c_uuid:   !uuid "12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789abc"
        c_fixed:  !fixed "DEADBEEF"             # hex, 4 bytes
        c_bin:    !binary "0102"
        c_str:    !string "alpha"
        c_struct: !struct { a: !int 7, b: !string "nested" }
        c_list:   !list [ !string "p", !string "q" ]
        c_map:    !map { "k1": !long 1, "k2": !long 2 }
      - __rowkey: r2
        c_int:    !int 2
        c_long:   !null null
        c_dec:    !decimal "0.01"
        c_ts:     !timestamp "2026-06-30T23:59:59"
        c_uuid:   !uuid "abcdefab-cdef-abcd-efab-cdefabcdefab"
        c_fixed:  !fixed "00000000"
        c_bin:    !binary "FF"
        c_str:    !string "alfredo"             # same truncate[2] prefix "al" as r1
        c_struct: !struct { a: !null null, b: !string "" }
        c_list:   !list []
        c_map:    !map {}
    expect:
      snapshot: { operation: append }

  # rule: mutations assert snapshot facts; observes assert row-state.
  - op: observe
    at: latest
    expect:
      scan:
        rows:
          - __rowkey: r1
            c_int: 1
            c_long: 10000000000
            c_dec: "12.34"
            c_str: alpha
            c_struct: { a: 7, b: nested }
            c_list: [ p, q ]
            c_map: { k1: 1, k2: 2 }
          - __rowkey: r2
            c_int: 2
            c_long: null
            c_dec: "0.01"
            c_str: alfredo
            c_struct: { a: null, b: "" }
            c_list: []
            c_map: {}
      physical:
        # both rows share truncate[2] "al" but differ on bucket[4](c_int);
        # pins that two distinct partition tuples exist and are layout-independent.
        partitions-distinct: ">= 1"
